text: The circle wins. No corners means no decisions about where to stop, no awkward transitions, no hierarchy of sides. It is complete in every direction simultaneously. Roll a circle and it keeps going. Spin it and nothing changes. Every point on its edge sits at exactly the same distance from the center, which is the kind of fairness most things only pretend to have. The circle appears in eyes, in planets, in the cross-section of every tree. Nature reaches for it when efficiency matters. Humans reach for it when they want to signal wholeness. Both instincts point the same direction.
